items.title: The BRCA is ready to become law
items.desc: The BRCA’s final text reflects the concerns of skeptics while preserving the protection non-custodial developers need. The post The BRCA is ready to become law appeared first on Coin Center.

items.title: Making stablecoins stable(r): can regulation help?
items.desc: by Tirupam Goel, Ulf Lewrick, Isha AgarwalWe model a stablecoin issuer that optimises capital, cash and bond holdings under persistent stablecoin flows. Absent regulation, the issuer holds little capital and favours interest-bearing but less-liquid bonds over cash.

items.title: BTC, ETH, SOL and XRP ETFs bleed $4.4 billion over 13 sessions, only HYPE in green
items.desc: BlackRock's IBIT shed another $342 million on Wednesday as ether, solana and XRP funds joined the redemption wave, leaving Hyperliquid's HYPE products as the only major crypto ETF category still pulling in net new money.

items.title: Bitmine eyes dividend-paying preferred shares, echoing Strategy’s playbook
items.desc: The move comes as Ether prices slumped to a 14-month low below $1,750 on Thursday.

items.title: Coinbase freezes $3M tied to Southeast Asia crypto fraud networks
items.desc: Authorities around the world have been heavily targeting scam infrastructure this year, with joint actions involving the US, UAE, China, Austria and Albania.

items.title: Polymarket’s UMA vote upholds ‘No’ outcome on Strategy bitcoin sale market despite backlash
items.desc: The dispute centers on Strategy's disclosure that it sold 32 BTC for roughly $2.5 million between May 26 and May 31.

items.title: CFTC follows SEC in scrapping ‘no-deny’ policy for settlements
items.desc: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig says the rescission of its “no-deny” policy means it now has more flexibility when settling enforcement actions.

items.title: Live Markets: Bitcoin bounces to $64,000 in classic oversold rebound
items.desc: Bitcoin rebounded to $64,000 after a sharp sell-off, with the move reflecting a typical bounce from oversold conditions rather than a confirmed trend reversal.

items.title: Ethereum Researchers Lay Out Post-Quantum Key Registry as First Concrete Migration Step
items.desc: A team led by Thomas Coratger and Justin Drake published the design for a dedicated XMSS public-key registry, the first protocol fork on the EF Strawmap before validators swap off BLS signatures.

items.title: Bitcoin tanks below $63,000 for the first time since February as price selloff deepens
items.desc: The selloff has triggered demand for protective options plays, pushing the fear gauge higher.

items.title: OppFi Ruling Gives Bank-FinTech Partnerships a Win but Not Final Clarity
items.desc: A California court has handed bank-FinTech partnerships one of their most significant legal victories to date. On May 19, Los Angeles County Superior Court Judge Gary D. Roberts ruled in favor of OppFi in its dispute with California’s Department of Financial Protection and Innovation (DFPI), finding that FinWise Bank was the lender in the […] The post OppFi Ruling Gives Bank-FinTech Partnerships a Win but Not Final Clarity appeared first on PYMNTS.com.

items.title: Israel’s tax authority ‘disappointed’ in voluntary crypto disclosures: Report
items.desc: After expecting billions of dollars worth of crypto holdings to be reported during a voluntary disclosure period, the country's tax office said only 58 filers took advantage of the procedure.

items.title: Revolut US bank plans stablecoins alongside FDIC-insured accounts: Report
items.desc: Reuters reported that Revolut plans to integrate stablecoins into its future US bank as more fintech and crypto companies chase federal banking approvals.

items.title: Cardano Slumps to 5-Year Low Price as Charles Hoskinson Warns of 'Wave of Failures'
items.desc: Founder Charles Hoskinson was brutally honest about the troubles the Cardano ecosystem could face as the market continues declining.

items.title: Deel Deploys Stripe's Full Stablecoin Stack to Pay 1.5M Contractors in DLUSD
items.desc: Deel is the first enterprise to combine Bridge, Privy, and Tempo in a single product, issuing a custom USD-denominated stablecoin for 1.5 million contractors across 150 countries, starting in Argentina.

items.title: PENDLE Token Goes Live on Revolut, Reaching 20M EEA Crypto Traders
items.desc: The yield-tokenization protocol's $233M-mcap token is now buyable inside Revolut's MiCA-licensed crypto app across the UK, EU, Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein — the second on-chain yield protocol to land on the fintech's regulated rails after Maple's SYRUP in April.

items.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
items.desc: Find insight on Canada’s online-streaming rules, Rogers Communications, bitcoin and more in the latest Market Talks covering technology, media and telecom.

items.title: The Crypto Winter Turns Colder as Bitcoin Extends Its Slide
items.desc: The digital token fell for a fourth straight day after a sale by one of bitcoin’s biggest cheerleaders.

items.title: Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson said traditional finance resists public blockchains because they threaten fee-based revenue, as the firm expands aggressively into tokenization, bitcoin products, and on-chain finance. This post Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitmine's Ethereum bet nears $9 billion loss as ether falls below $1,800
items.desc: Shares of Tom Lee's Ethereum treasury firm hit their lowest level since the company's crypto pivot as ETH revisits the February lows.

items.title: Someone Just Redeemed a 15-Year-Old Physical Bitcoin, Scoring $1.78 Million in BTC
items.desc: Physical Casascius coins were minted 15 years ago in the early days of Bitcoin—and the BTC held within is worth a whole lot more today.

items.title: Revolut Plans 2027 US Bank Launch With Stablecoin Services Built In From Day One
items.desc: The British neobank, with 70 million customers and a $75 billion valuation, will pair FDIC-insured accounts with stablecoin access on a single platform — a tier-one fintech entering US banking with stablecoins designed in, not bolted on.

items.title: Builder-Deployed Perp Markets Push Hyperliquid to Record Share of Global Perps Volume
items.desc: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 framework — which lets anyone deploy a perpetual futures market on the protocol's settlement layer — cleared $62 billion in May volume, pushing the venue to a record 6.63% share of global perps and 14.4% against Binance.

items.title: Crypto VC deal count slumps to five-year low as investors grow more selective
items.desc: Monthly venture deal count in crypto fell to roughly 50 deals in May, a level not seen since before 2021.

items.title: House of Lords calls for BofE to reconsider stablecoin plans
items.desc: A House of Lords committee has urged the Bank of England to loosen its proposals for stablecoin regulation, adding that the UK is lagging the US and EU in developing its regime.

items.title: Bitcoin ATMs: The Canary in the Coal Mine
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin ATMs: The Canary in the Coal Mine State regulators have begun quietly implementing regulations to ban or overburden Bitcoin ATM operators. This post Bitcoin ATMs: The Canary in the Coal Mine first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Michelle Weekley.

items.title: The 2036 Issue: Nobody Even Noticed
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine The 2036 Issue: Nobody Even Noticed From The 2036 Issue: A possible roadmap for Bitcoin's adoption as a real means of payment, by David Marcus, CEO of Lightspark. This post The 2036 Issue: Nobody Even Noticed first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by David Marcus.

items.title: World Cup Crypto Scams Are Targeting Soccer Fans, Law Enforcement Warns
items.desc: Law enforcement agencies are warning soccer fans of fake ticket sites and crypto payment schemes around the World Cup.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $66,000 as Spot ETF Outflow Streak Hits a Record 12 Days
items.desc: US spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled in another $519 million of net redemptions on June 2, extending the longest outflow run since the funds launched and tipping a leveraged market into $1.86 billion of forced selling.

items.title: EdgeX offers refunds and launches 200,000 USDC bounty after 71% token flash crash
items.desc: EdgeX says that 174 addresses flooded a PancakeSwap pool with EDGE token sell orders during a period of low liquidity.

items.title: Hyperliquid hits record share of global perps market as HIP-3 tops $62 billion monthly volume
items.desc: Despite the bullish sentiment on HIP-3, Hyperliquid's pure crypto volumes are down significantly year over year.

items.title: Stripe Millionaire Loses Bid for Congress to Candidate Backed by Ripple Co-Founder
items.desc: Former Stripe engineer Saikat Chakrabarti lost his bid to succeed Nancy Pelosi in California to a candidate backed by Ripple's Chris Larsen.

items.title: Cardano's TapTools Winding Down is a Symptom of a Shrinking Chain
items.desc: TapTools, the most-used analytics platform on Cardano, said on X it will wind down within two weeks after losing its fifth senior executive in a year. Founder Charles Hoskinson warned in a video the same day that more older Cardano-ecosystem projects will fold in the second half of 2026.

items.title: Bessent backs summer push for Clarity Act, says bitcoin reserve moving at ‘deliberate speed’
items.desc: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the department is moving at a "deliberate speed" to establish a strategic bitcoin reserve.

items.title: Tether-Backed Adecoagro to Launch Sugarcane-Powered Bitcoin Mining in Brazil
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Tether-Backed Adecoagro to Launch Sugarcane-Powered Bitcoin Mining in Brazil Adecoagro is set to begin Bitcoin mining operations in Brazil using electricity generated from sugarcane waste, with a target launch date of July 1. This post Tether-Backed Adecoagro to Launch Sugarcane-Powered Bitcoin Mining in Brazil first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: What about the American consumer?
items.desc: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Alex Tapscott on the stalling of the CLARITY Act and how it's impacting the average American consumer. Then, Aisha Hunt writes that crypto will grow by upgrading Wall Street’s trusted products rather than replacing them.

items.title: Bitcoin copying 2022 'almost perfectly' as trader sees key support failing
items.desc: Bitcoin traders warned of a key BTC price support trend line failing as 2022 bear market history continued to repeat.

items.title: Crypto firms face July 1 EU cutoff as MiCA grace period ends
items.desc: The ESMA says crypto companies without MiCA authorization must stop serving EU clients from July 1, even if their licence applications remain under review.

items.title: Hyperliquid bear turns bullish after losing over $46M shorting HYPE
items.desc: The whale is now backing Arthur Hayes’ favored HYPE, ZEC and NEAR plays, signaling a sharp pivot toward momentum-led crypto bets.

items.title: Solayer rolls out Solana-native onchain perps trading platform Margin Trade mainnet
items.desc: Margin Trade enables trading of crypto, commodities, and equities via a single margin account on Solana, tapping the SVM chain Solayer.

items.title: Sen. Lummis says JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon hasn’t read Clarity Act, calls remarks ‘distasteful’
items.desc: Republican Senator Lummis said the JPMorgan CEO "either hasn't read" the Clarity Act "or he wants to mislead people."

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Lava Card Launches Secured Visa Credit Card That Pays Bitcoin Rewards on Every Purchase
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Lava Card Launches Secured Visa Credit Card That Pays Bitcoin Rewards on Every Purchase Lava has launched a secured Visa credit card that lets users fund spending with cash or stablecoins while earning Bitcoin rewards on every purchase. This post Lava Card Launches Secured Visa Credit Card That Pays Bitcoin Rewards on Every Purchase first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Every single bank will soon need to hold digital assets, says Zodia CEO Julian Sawyer
items.desc: Sawyer confirmed that Standard Chartered’s full acquisition of the firm is on track to target a signing at the end of June and complete by the end of August.

items.title: Zcash Completes 'Most Ambitious' Network Upgrade as ZEC Resumes Recent Surge
items.desc: A Zcash vulnerability could have allowed double-spending within the network's flagship privacy pool, though no exploitation occurred.

items.title: Binance discloses revenue-sharing deal with Alpaca
items.desc: A Binance legal document disclosed a revenue-sharing agreement with Alpaca, which includes sharing 50% of the stock custodian’s order flow revenue with the cryptocurrency exchange.

items.title: Revolut’s US bank to offer stablecoin services alongside FDIC-insured products: Reuters
items.desc: Revolut U.S. CEO Cetin Duransoy told Reuters the company plans to offer access to stablecoins in the United States.

items.title: Binance to end NFT support on exchange, shift service to wallet
items.desc: Binance exchange said it will move NFT support from its exchange to its non-custodial wallet and offered users 30 days to migrate their NFTs.

items.title: Zcash fixes Orchard bug after emergency network upgrade
items.desc: Zcash Open Development Lab said the network briefly became unstable as miners upgraded, while the Zcash Foundation said there was no evidence of an exploit.

items.title: Rare physical bitcoin worth $1.78 million gets cashed in after 12 years
items.desc: The S1-COIN-25 physical bitcoin, part of Mike Caldwell's 2011-2013 mint, had its tamper-evident hologram peeled and the 25 BTC swept on-chain on Wednesday.

items.title: Tether launching tokenized gold Visa card with XAUT rewards
items.desc: The card will operate on the Visa network, enabling users to spend fiat at merchant stores worldwide where Visa cards are accepted.

items.title: Trezor says Safe 7 chip flaw found by Ledger does not put funds at risk
items.desc: Trezor and Tropic Square disclosed a TROPIC01 chip vulnerability found during a Ledger Donjon audit, saying the Safe 7 wallet and user funds remain secure.

items.title: Old DxSale Lockers Drained for $7.3M Across 1,400 BNB Chain Pools as Owner-Privilege Exploits Pile Up
items.desc: A dormant launchpad contract from 2021 was emptied this week through a quiet ownership transfer and a one-wei fee reset — the latest in a string of BNB Chain drains that turn admin keys into the attack surface.

items.title: Mastercard Opens Card-Settlement Network on Eight Blockchains, Adding Weekend and Holiday Cycles
items.desc: Issuers and acquirers can now clear card transactions in regulated stablecoins on Arbitrum, Base, Canton, Ethereum, Polygon, Solana, Tempo and XRPL, with ARQ, CBW Bank, Cross River, Lead Bank and Nuvei lined up as the first adopters in the US and Latin America.

items.title: Binance Closes Its Centralized NFT Marketplace, Joining Coinbase, Kraken in CEX-Backed NFT Retreat
items.desc: The world's largest crypto exchange is folding its in-house NFT service into Binance Wallet by July 3, ending the centralized-marketplace bet a year after Coinbase NFT shut down and four months after X2Y2's sunset — a near-complete unwinding of the CEX-backed NFT marketplace category.

items.title: Russian ruble stablecoin A7A5 grows despite Western sanctions: CertiK
items.desc: Russian ruble-backed stablecoin A7A5 processed over $110 billion in transactions despite Western sanctions, according to CertiK.

items.title: Payments Canada welcomes 15 new members
items.desc: Payments Canada announced today the approval of new members including Beem Credit Union, Ebury Partners Canada Limited (“Ebury”), Shaype Canada Financial Limited (“Shaype”), Libro Credit Union and Newton Crypto Ltd.

items.title: Bitcoin's dearth of fresh investors matters more than Strategy's sale, Citi says
items.desc: Strategy's bitcoin sale may have rattled markets, but the bigger issue is missing demand from new buyers, Citi said.

items.title: Bitwise model puts bitcoin fair value at $224,000 as sovereign-default hedge
items.desc: The figure is from a theoretical model proposed by Greg Foss in 2021 that treats bitcoin as a credit default swap on G20 sovereign bonds.

items.title: UK regulator warns Premier League clubs over unauthorized crypto sponsors
items.desc: The FCA has urged clubs to rethink deals with unauthorized crypto and trading sponsors, warning that fans risk losing all their money and teams face legal and reputational fallout.

items.title: Clarity Act survival depends on the U.S. Senate getting a lot of non-crypto work done
items.desc: The dwindling congressional calendar may spur a legislative competition for the crypto market structure bill to win Senate floor time against other priorities.

items.title: House of Lords committee urges UK regulators to ease stablecoin rules that could stifle market growth
items.desc: A UK House of Lords committee warned that Britain lags the U.S. and the EU on stablecoins, calling on the BoE and FCA to revise rules.

items.title: Trezor Reveals Hardware Wallet Vulnerability, But Funds 'Safe'
items.desc: The vulnerability in Trezor's TROPIC01 Secure Element chip was uncovered by an audit carried out by the Ledger Donjon team.

items.title: 160 Officials Tell Senate to Pass CLARITY Act as Floor Talks Resume
items.desc: A Blockchain Association letter signed by ex-CIA, DOJ, Treasury, FBI, IRS-CI, Secret Service and military officials lands on Thune and Schumer the same day the Senate restarts crypto market-structure negotiations.

items.title: Marex arrives on crypto option exchange Deribit
items.desc: Marex Group plc (NASDAQ: MRX), the diversified global financial services platform, today announces that it has been onboarded as a broker on Deribit, the leading crypto options exchange, further expanding Marex’s institutional crypto offering and enhancing client access to global digital asset markets.

items.title: RedotPay enters B2B market
items.desc: RedotPay, a global stablecoin-based payment fintech company serving over 7 million users across 100+ countries, today announced the launch of RedotPay Connect at Money20/20 Europe, marking the company’s first dedicated B2B product since inception and a major step into enterprise payments.

items.title: Bitcoin hits Power Law level low that historically precedes a rebound
items.desc: The power law model shows BTC trading at one of its deepest discounts relative to trend, a level previously seen during the March 2020 crash and FTX collapse.

items.title: Grayscale gets behind the hype, launching its HYPG Hyperliquid ETF on Nasdaq
items.desc: Grayscale entered the ring with its own Hyperliquid linked ETF, setting a new low for fees as competition intensifies among firms.

items.title: Grayscale launches lowest-fee U.S. Hyperliquid ETF as competition heats up around HYPE
items.desc: The Nasdaq-listed fund charges a 0.29% sponsor fee, undercutting rival Hyperliquid ETFs from 21Shares and Bitwise.

items.title: Mastercard expands support to USDC, PYUSD, RLUSD stablecoin settlement
items.desc: The payments giant said USDC, PYUSD, RLUSD and other stablecoins will support new settlement options across multiple blockchains.

items.title: Payment giants Stripe, Visa, Mastercard said to be among backers of soon-to-debut stablecoin platform
items.desc: U.S. crypto exchange Coinbase is also said to be looking into the possibility of participating in the new stablecoin platform.

items.title: Crypto PACs go undefeated in June primaries as Fairshake scores bipartisan winning streak
items.desc: Winners included Democrats Zoe Lofgren, Ted Lieu, Dave Min and Rob Menendez, as well as Republican Sen. Mike Rounds, with Democrats accounting for 10 of the 11 victories.

items.title: Bitcoin momentum gauge hints at recovery. Experts remain cautious.
items.desc: Your day-ahead look for June 3, 2026

items.title: Bitcoin's crash to $65K triggers $1.8B in crypto liquidations
items.desc: Bitcoin's drop to $65,000 triggered over $1.8 billion in crypto liquidations as traders brace for a test of $60,000 as support.

items.title: New York and EU’s finance watchdogs team up to police stablecoins
items.desc: Some of the information that New York and the European Union’s watchdogs will share includes the issued stablecoins, total volume in circulation and the number of holders.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $66K as US and Iran launch new strikes
items.desc: Bitcoin saw its largest daily drop since early February on Tuesday as the cryptocurrency shed more than $4,500 in a single day.

items.title: US Treasury issues sanctions on Iran, targets 4 crypto exchanges
items.desc: The sanctions come four days after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US had seized nearly $1 billion in crypto from Iranian crypto exchanges and wallets since late February.

items.title: Cardsmiths' New America250 Trading Cards Have Real Bitcoin, Dogecoin Up for Grabs
items.desc: Cardsmiths’ latest Currency trading card series tackles American history—and some packs include codes for real Bitcoin, Ethereum, and more.

items.title: US Treasury Sanctions Iranian Crypto Exchanges Including Nobitex for Terrorist Financing
items.desc: The Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control accused the platforms of enabling illicit finance activities across Iran's crypto ecosystem.

items.title: As Bitcoin Sinks, It's Time for Ethereum to Outperform: Standard Chartered
items.desc: Bitcoin is falling following Strategy's first BTC sale since 2022—and one analyst sees that as a good thing for Ethereum.

items.title: Franklin Templeton Wires BENJI Money-Market Fund Into MoonPay Trade for Onchain Stablecoin Swaps
items.desc: The $1.74 trillion asset manager is letting institutions move directly between stablecoins and shares of its tokenized US government money fund through MoonPay's new institutional rails, marking one of the first listings of a US-registered tokenized MMF on a third-party onchain trading venue.

items.title: Sui Blames Triple Mainnet Halt on Gas-Charging Bug and a Known-Risk Patch That Backfired
items.desc: The Sui Foundation's post-mortem ties two of three outages to an edge case in the v1.72 address-balances feature, and the third to a randomness-state bug exposed by an interim fix the team shipped knowing it carried halt risk.

items.title: US Treasury Adds Nobitex and Three Other Iranian Exchanges to OFAC SDN List Under 'Economic Fury'
items.desc: The Office of Foreign Assets Control designated Nobitex, Wallex, Bitpin and Ramzinex, along with their leadership, naming the exchanges as the digital-asset rails for Iran's central bank and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

items.title: Polymarket Books First On-Chain Institutional Block Trade
items.desc: A six-figure trade settled against the Ornn Compute Price Index pushes the world's largest prediction market into TradFi-style risk transfer, with FalconX signing on as a dedicated market maker for future blocks.

items.title: Where Does Bitcoin Go From Here? This Is What the Charts Say
items.desc: Bitcoin just had its worst day since April. The death cross is in effect, and prediction markets are bearish.

items.title: New York’s Crypto Watchdog Teams With EU to Police Stablecoins
items.desc: The New York Department of Financial Services and European Banking Authority said they'll work together to oversee stablecoins.

items.title: Bad Sentiment, Strong Fundamentals: the Institutional Turn | Chris Perkins, Franklin Crypto
items.desc: Crypto is entering a new phase — and institutions, not retail, are driving it. As regulatory clarity arrives, the largest institutions in the world are moving from the sidelines into the market. The thesis from our guest: sentiment is poor, but fundamentals are improving; a coiled spring.

items.title: Coinbase Ventures Buys ENA on the Open Market as Coinbase and Ethena Strike Distribution Deal
items.desc: The exchange's venture arm bought ENA tokens directly on the open market rather than through a private round, the first such disclosed purchase, alongside a partnership to push Ethena's onchain dollar and savings products into Coinbase's userbase.

items.title: US Court Lifts Circle Freeze on Zama's $12.5M cUSDC Contract After Three-Day Lockout
items.desc: A federal judge in the Northern District of California reversed the temporary restraining order that had pushed Circle to blacklist the privacy protocol's wrapper, restoring funds to depositors caught in a civil dispute they were not party to.

items.title: Securitize Brings Hamilton Lane's HLSCOPE Private-Credit Fund to TRON in First Issuance on the Network
items.desc: The tokenized Senior Credit Opportunities feeder fund — already live on Polygon, Ethereum and three other chains — becomes the inaugural Securitize-issued asset on a network with 383 million accounts and roughly $90 billion in circulating stablecoins.

items.title: OpenSea Teases Hyperliquid-Powered Perpetuals Launch
items.desc: OpenSea Product Marketing Lead Zack Brenner asked users on X who wanted early access to perpetual contracts, then confirmed the product would run on Hyperliquid's builder-code rails — the clearest signal yet the NFT marketplace is bolting on a derivatives venue.

items.title: Robinhood Closes $180M WonderFi Deal, Crossing 1M International Customers as It Enters Canada
items.desc: The retail brokerage's first Canadian footprint comes via a year-late buyout of the operator of Bitbuy and Coinsquare, adding roughly 300,000 funded crypto customers in a single transaction.

items.title: MoneyGram Launches MGUSD Stablecoin on Stellar
items.desc: The Dallas-based payments company is now the issuer of a U.S. dollar token on a public chain, with Stripe-owned Bridge as the regulated minter, M0 smart contracts handling supply, and Fireblocks holding the float.
